title: ABI Sanger Sequencing of Avian Clock genes to elucidate markers for Migration Phenology
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"764vm","text":"This protocol follows up on \"PCR Amplification of Clock genes with EmeraldAmp® GT PCR Master Mix in Avian species\" and is intended to provide the next steps used in the sanger sequencing of the produced amplicons. This protocol uses ABI BigDye reagents (but suitable alternatives exist). The same primers used to produce the PCR products, of the respective clock genes, are used individually in a forward and reverse sequencing reaction. Cycling conditions mimic those used for PCR. Sequencing reactions are purified and subsequently resolved on an ABI Genetic Analyzer. The sequence read data was used in a BLAST search and confirmed to be the genes and regions of interest for all tested species.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare BigDye™ Terminator v3.1 Cycle Sequencing Kit Applied Biosystems (ThermoFisher Scientific) Master mix and Samples* for Sanger Sequencing. *Sample information has been deposited to BioSample and associated to the BioProject ( PRJNA737185 ) which used this protocol. (An experiment template is included as an excel spreadsheet)

Program and run the following cycle conditions on a thermal cycler, e.g. SimpliAmp Thermal Cycler (PCR, Applied Biosystems, A24811, Any standard PCR thermocycler will suffice, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/A24811 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/9df3sqe.jpeg ) Initial denaturation at 96 °C for 00:01:00 25 cycles of: Denaturation at 96 °C for 00:00:10 Annealing at 50 °C for 00:00:05 Extension at 60 °C for 00:02:00 Hold at 4 °C until next step.
Purify the sequencing products using BigDye XTerminator™ Purification Kit Thermo Fisher Catalog #4376484

Load sequencing reactions to sequencing plate and set up a run on the genetic analyzer, e.g. Genetic analyser with plates loaded for capillary electrophoresis. 3500 Genetic Analyzer (Sequence analyzer, Applied Biosystems, 4440470, DNA sequence fragment analysis, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/4440470?SID=srch-hj-4440470#/4440470?SID=srch-hj-4440470 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/durpbkcsp.webp ) Set up plate layout for fragment analysis on genetic analyser. Export sequence read trace files once done! Files can be opened with BioEdit 7.2.6.1 Windows 10 32-bit Informer Tom Hall or MEGA X or Sequence Scanner 2 Windows 10 32-bit Informer Life Technologies View of trace file in Seq Scanner 2

Prepare the following sequencing master mixes in duplicate, one for each primer (forward and reverse). Master Mix: A B C D 1 BigDye™ 3.1 Ready MM X2.5 X1 4 2 Primer 3.2 µM 3.2 pM 1 3 Nuclease free water - - 4 Components of sequencing reaction, indicating stock and final concentrations as well as the relative volume needed in microliters. (for a 20μL reaction you can double the volume of each component) Plate set up for Sanger sequencing.
Add 9 µL, Master Mix to 1 µL, purified PCR product to the individual wells of a 96-well PCR plate or thin-walled PCR tubes.
Vortex the bottle of BigDye XTerminator™ beads for 8 to 10 seconds before mixing with the SAM solution.
Prepare the SAM/BigDye XTerminator™ bead working solution: Volumes of SAM solution and beads to add
Transfer the indicated volume of bead mix (BigDye XTerminator™ bead solution and SAM solution) to each.

Vortex the 96-well plate/tubes at 1800 rpm, 21 °C, 20 minutes on a shaker, e.g. IKA MS 3 Digital Vortex Mixer (Vortex mixer, IKA, 3319000, Vortex mixing of plates, https://www.m2sci.com/ika-ms-3-digital-vortex-mixer/ , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/durmbkcsp.jpg , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/durnbkcsp.pdf )
In a swinging bucket centrifuge, centrifuge the plate at 1000 x g, 21 °C, 2 minutes go to step # .
creator: Louis-Stéphane Le Clercq
